rows.notes: Page title at top. Handwriting legible and no amounts on this line. 'Arrearages recipienda 1789.' read clearly though final letter forms are slightly stylized.
Clear lines align with the right-hand £ s d columns. Pounds '23', shillings '15', pence '11' are crisp and within valid ranges.
Pounds '05' (leading zero), shillings '19', pence '0' clearly aligned. Minor uncertainty in the first word of description (Gavington vs Garrington) but amount clear.
Amounts '11 18 0' are well-aligned and legible. Description first word uncertain (read as 'Prickett'); 'Do.' and 'pro bien' clear.

Pounds and shillings ('05' and '1') align and are legible. Pence appears as '1' with a small trailing mark interpreted as a farthing (0.25) rather than a halfpenny; the fraction glyph is faint and could be a loop, so fraction interpretation is uncertain.
Amounts '10 6 0' are clear and perfectly aligned with the right columns. 'pro 12 annis' reading is clear; given name 'Sam. Benton' read with good certainty.
Amounts read as '11 5 4' — pounds and shillings crisp. Pence digit (4) is slightly cramped but consistent with column alignment; no fractional mark visible.
Large pounds figure '113' is clear and well aligned with the pounds column. Shillings '16' and pence '0' unambiguous.
Amounts '01 16 0' clear with a leading zero on pounds. Description legible as 'S. North Ten. pro an.'
Amounts '33 11 7' are well aligned. Description 'Chace Hill pro 7 annis' read clearly; the numeral '7' (annis) is the only minor ambiguity but context confirms.
Amounts '64 12 11' clearly aligned and legible. Description reads 'Benside' though initial letter has a flourish; amount unambiguous.
Amounts '30 0 0' clear. Description is slightly contracted ('pro dim. an.') but the entry and amounts are unambiguous.
Amounts '20 0 0' clearly written. The phrase 'in pecun.' is legible and confirms the entry; column alignment is precise.

The left description begins with two dash marks then 'Vic pro an' (interpreted as an abbreviated entry). Amounts read as '04 0 0' — pounds '4' clear; the description portion is abbreviated and somewhat ambiguous.

Amounts '07 10 0' are legible. Description handwriting is awkward and the words read as 'Thump an. gdim.' — the precise words are uncertain so confidence lowered for the description while amounts are clear.
Amounts '06 0 0' clear. Description likely 'Lane, Eaton' with small uncertainty on final word 'pro tien' but readable enough.

Amounts '17 13 4' align and are legible. The description contains abbreviations and numerals '3' and 'Lent.' which are somewhat ambiguous in the handwriting; chosen reading fits the visible letters but alternative transcriptions plausible.
Amounts '06 0 0' clear. 'Montacute pro 12 annis' is legible in the description; the year/count '12' is clear.
Amounts '01 0 0' clear. Description 'Bampton pro an.' easily readable.
Amounts '06 13 4' visible but pence digit is slightly cramped. Place name read as 'Medunham' though alternate spellings possible (e.g., 'Medenham'); amounts are the main reliable element.

rows.description: Sir G. P. Turner pro an
rows.notes: Amounts '06 10 10' are clearly aligned. Title and name 'Sir G.P. Turner' are legible though punctuation is handwritten; reading confident.
Amounts '05 14 0' clear. Abbreviated place 'St. Mark. Par.' and '3 an.' are legible and match format of other entries.

rows.description: St. Mar. Magd. Do. pro 2 an.
rows.notes: Amounts '02 16 8' are clear. Description contains abbreviations ('St. Mar. Magd.') but is legible; 'pro 2 an.' also clear.

Pounds and shillings '28 14' clear. Pence appears as '5' with a small halfpenny mark (loop) after it — interpreted as 5½ (0.5). The fractional glyph is faint but its position and shape match other halfpenny markings on similar documents.

rows.description: De Tenements in Lew parva pro anno.
rows.notes: No amounts visible at far right for this final line; appears to be an entry header/subgroup ('De Tenements in Lew parva pro anno.') with no numeric value on this line. Handwriting for description is readable though slightly faint.
